skill.name: opencontext
skill.description: Persistent memory and context management for AI agents using OpenContext. Keep context across sessions/repos/dates, store conclusions, and provide document search workflows.
skill.summary: Persistent memory and context management for AI agents using OpenContext.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 101 patterns but all are false positives. The skill is documentation-only (SKILL.md) using markdown code blocks for examples, not executable code. Documented paths (~/.opencontext/, ~/.claude/) are installation locations, not malicious file access. URLs are reference links to OpenContext resources. No actual code execution, network calls, or credential access exists in this skill.

content.user_title: Enable Persistent Memory for AI Agents
content.value_statement: AI assistants lose context across sessions, repos, and dates, forcing users to repeat background and re-explain decisions. OpenContext provides a lightweight context store that preserves project knowledge, decisions, and conclusions for consistent multi-session collaboration.
actual_capabilities: Store and retrieve project context across AI sessions
Create and organize documents with folders and metadata
Search documents using keyword, hybrid, or vector modes
Generate manifests listing relevant files for AI reading
Create stable document links that survive file renames
Support multi-agent workflows with shared knowledge base
limitations: Requires OpenContext CLI installation (npm package or npx)
Vector search requires embedding API key and index building
Desktop app features require separate download and setup
Slash commands must be installed to each AI tool's command directory

use_cases.title: Software Development Teams
use_cases.description: Maintain project documentation, API contracts, and architectural decisions in a searchable knowledge base. New team members can quickly onboard by searching prior conclusions.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering teams using AI assistants for development

use_cases.title: Multi-Agent Workflows
use_cases.description: Coordinate between Claude for planning, Gemini for analysis, and Codex for execution. Store intermediate results and final conclusions in OpenContext for seamless handoffs.
use_cases.target_user: Advanced users orchestrating multiple AI agents

use_cases.title: Solo Developers
use_cases.description: Build personal project memory that persists across days and weeks. Record decisions, pitfalls, and next steps so you never lose context when returning to a project.
use_cases.target_user: Individual developers using AI pair programming

prompt_templates.title: Load Project Context Before Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Load the project context from OpenContext for the current repository. Show me the project background, known pitfalls, and any relevant prior decisions before I start working.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new work session on an existing project

prompt_templates.title: Search Prior Conclusions
prompt_templates.prompt: Search OpenContext for documents related to "API authentication patterns". Summarize what we have already decided and link to the relevant source documents.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researching existing decisions before making changes

prompt_templates.title: Store Implementation Decisions
prompt_templates.prompt: After completing the refactoring task, create a new OpenContext document summarizing the decisions made, patterns used, and any pitfalls discovered. Include code examples and link to related documents.
prompt_templates.scenario: Documenting work完成后 for future reference

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Agent Analysis Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: 1. Search OpenContext for existing codebase analysis. 2. If incomplete, use Gemini to analyze the src/ directory structure. 3. Store new findings in OpenContext with citations. 4. Generate a manifest for Claude to read before implementation.
prompt_templates.scenario: Coordinating analysis across multiple AI agents

output_examples.output:
Project: API Redesign
Background: Migrating from REST to GraphQL. Team decided on Apollo Server with schema-first approach.
Known Pitfalls: Legacy /v1 endpoints must remain during transition. Rate limiting is per-IP, not per-user.
Recent Decisions: Chose DataLoader for batching. Authentication via JWT with 15-minute expiry.
Next Steps: Complete user resolvers, add mutation tests, deploy to staging.

Found 3 documents:
1. Migration Plan (doc_id: a7f3) - Covers zero-downtime approach using dual-write pattern
2. Rollback Procedure (doc_id: b2e1) - Steps to revert if migration fails
3. Testing Checklist (doc_id: c9d4) - Pre-deployment validation steps

best_practices: Load context at session start with /opencontext-context to ensure the AI has full project background
Document decisions immediately after completing work while details are fresh
Use consistent folder structure to organize documents by feature or component
anti_patterns: Storing sensitive data like API keys or credentials in OpenContext documents
Creating overly broad documents that mix unrelated topics and become hard to search
Forgetting to update documents when project decisions change, leading to stale information

faq.question: What is OpenContext and how does it work?
faq.answer: OpenContext is a CLI tool and document store that helps AI assistants maintain persistent memory. It stores documents in a local database (~/.opencontext) and provides slash commands to load context, search documents, and store conclusions during your AI sessions.

faq.question: Do I need to install the OpenContext CLI to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. Install via npm with 'npm install -g @aicontextlab/cli' or use npx for one-off commands. Run 'oc init' inside your project to set up the local context store.

faq.question: Which AI platforms support OpenContext integration?
faq.answer: OpenContext works with Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Gemini, and ChatGPT. Slash commands install to platform-specific directories (~/.claude/commands, ~/.cursor/commands, etc.).

faq.question: Is vector search required for OpenContext to work?
faq.answer: No. Keyword search works without any setup. Vector search requires an embedding API key and running 'oc index build' but provides better semantic matching for complex queries.

faq.question: Can multiple AI agents share the same OpenContext?
faq.answer: Yes. OpenContext is designed for multi-agent workflows. Different AI assistants can read from and write to the same context store, enabling knowledge sharing between planning, analysis, and execution agents.

faq.question: Where are my OpenContext documents stored?
faq.answer: Documents are stored in ~/.opencontext/contexts (individual context folders) and indexed in ~/.opencontext/opencontext.db (SQLite database). The Desktop App provides a UI to browse and edit documents.
